name: Fettuccine Alfredo II
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the semolina flour and all-purpose flour in a large bowl.
2. Mix well.
3. Make a well in the center of the flour mixture and add the eggs, olive oil, and salt.
4. Using a fork, beat the mixture into the flour, gradually incorporating the wet ingredients with the flour until thoroughly combined. If too dry, add a few drops of water.
5. When combined, moisten your hands with oil and work the dough, kneading and folding for about 10 minutes until smooth and pliable.
6. Continue kneading an additional 5 minutes until completely smooth (kneading will be difficult due to the toughness of semolina flour).
7. Form the dough into a ball, cover, and let rest for 10 minutes.
8. Process through a pasta machine according to the manufacturer's instructions or roll out very thin on a floured surface.
9. Cut the noodles into fettuccine shapes.
10. Use immediately or allow to dry and store for future use.
11. When ready to serve, drop the fettuccine into boiling salted water and cook until they float to the surface, about 2 minutes.
12. Quickly drain the pasta and place in a warm bowl containing half of the softened butter.
13. Top with lumps of remaining butter and 1/3 to 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese.
14. Toss lightly using a fork for about 2 minutes until the fettuccine is well coated and the creamy sauce forms.
15. Serve sprinkled with freshly ground black pepper and additional grated cheese.
